title: Airport
description: Essential vocabulary and phrases for traveling through airports

video.title: Airport English - Check In, Security, Boarding

vocabulary.meaning: A card that allows you to board an airplane
vocabulary.example: Please show your boarding pass at the gate.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of registering at the airport
vocabulary.example: Online check-in opens 24 hours before departure.

vocabulary.meaning: The act of leaving a place
vocabulary.example: The departure time is 10:30 AM.

vocabulary.meaning: The act of reaching a destination
vocabulary.example: The arrival hall is on the ground floor.

vocabulary.meaning: Bags and suitcases used for traveling
vocabulary.example: Please collect your luggage at carousel 3.

vocabulary.meaning: The place where officials check goods entering a country
vocabulary.example: You must declare items at customs.

vocabulary.meaning: A building at an airport where passengers arrive and depart
vocabulary.example: International flights depart from Terminal 2.

vocabulary.meaning: An official document for international travel
vocabulary.example: Make sure your passport is valid for at least 6 months.

vocabulary.meaning: The area where passengers board the plane
vocabulary.example: Boarding will begin at gate B12.

vocabulary.meaning: A person who serves passengers on an airplane
vocabulary.example: The flight attendant offered drinks to passengers.

vocabulary.meaning: When a flight is late or postponed
vocabulary.example: The flight has a 2-hour delay due to weather.

vocabulary.meaning: A stop between connecting flights
vocabulary.example: We have a 3-hour layover in Tokyo.

vocabulary.meaning: Storage compartment above seats on a plane
vocabulary.translation: ngan chua hanh ly tren cao
vocabulary.example: Please store your bag in the overhead bin.

vocabulary.meaning: The passage between rows of seats on a plane
vocabulary.example: I prefer an aisle seat for more legroom.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of officially entering a country
vocabulary.translation: nhap cu / kiem tra ho chieu
vocabulary.example: Please join the immigration line for foreign passports.

vocabulary.meaning: The strip of ground where planes take off and land
vocabulary.example: The plane taxied to the runway.

vocabulary.meaning: A machine capable of flight
vocabulary.example: The aircraft departed on time.

vocabulary.meaning: The person who flies an airplane
vocabulary.example: The pilot announced a smooth flight ahead.

vocabulary.meaning: The team of people working on an aircraft
vocabulary.example: The crew welcomed passengers on board.

vocabulary.meaning: A small bag brought onto the plane cabin
vocabulary.example: Only one carry-on bag is permitted.

vocabulary.meaning: A safety strap worn around the waist on a plane
vocabulary.example: Please fasten your seat belt during takeoff.

vocabulary.meaning: A fold-down table attached to the seat in front on a plane
vocabulary.example: Please fold up your tray table for landing.

vocabulary.meaning: A seat on a plane next to the window
vocabulary.example: I always request a window seat.

vocabulary.meaning: The checkpoint where passports are checked
vocabulary.example: Queue up at passport control when you arrive.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of checking passengers and bags for dangerous items
vocabulary.example: Remove your shoes at security screening.

vocabulary.meaning: A form listing goods you are bringing into a country
vocabulary.example: Fill in the declaration form before landing.

vocabulary.meaning: Goods sold without local taxes at the airport
vocabulary.example: I bought perfume at the duty-free shop.

vocabulary.meaning: The area where passengers collect their checked bags
vocabulary.example: Your bags will be at baggage claim area B.

vocabulary.meaning: The moving belt that delivers luggage at an airport
vocabulary.example: Wait at carousel 4 for your bags.

vocabulary.meaning: A service that lets you hire a car at the airport
vocabulary.example: We booked a car rental for our road trip.

vocabulary.meaning: A comfortable waiting area for passengers at the airport
vocabulary.example: Business class passengers have lounge access.

vocabulary.meaning: The right to board the plane before other passengers
vocabulary.example: Premium ticket holders get priority boarding.

vocabulary.meaning: A premium seating section with more comfort on a plane
vocabulary.example: Business class seats are wider and more comfortable.

vocabulary.meaning: The standard lowest-priced class on a plane
vocabulary.example: We flew economy class to save money.

vocabulary.meaning: A covered walkway connecting the airport to an aircraft
vocabulary.example: We walked to the plane via the air bridge.

vocabulary.meaning: Airport workers who handle baggage and aircraft on the ground
vocabulary.example: The ground crew loaded the bags quickly.

vocabulary.meaning: The desk at the airport where you register for your flight
vocabulary.example: Go to the check-in counter two hours before departure.

vocabulary.meaning: An official permit to enter a foreign country
vocabulary.example: I need a tourist visa to visit that country.

vocabulary.meaning: The scheduled time to start boarding the plane
vocabulary.example: Boarding time is 30 minutes before departure.

vocabulary.meaning: A code that identifies a specific flight
vocabulary.example: Your flight number is VN423.

vocabulary.meaning: The hard surface of an airport where planes move
vocabulary.example: We waited on the tarmac before takeoff.

vocabulary.meaning: When an airplane leaves the ground and begins to fly
vocabulary.example: Passengers must stay seated during takeoff.

vocabulary.meaning: When an airplane comes down and touches the ground
vocabulary.example: The landing was smooth despite the bad weather.

vocabulary.meaning: A document giving you the right to travel on a flight
vocabulary.example: I booked my ticket online three months in advance.

vocabulary.meaning: Luggage that exceeds the weight limit and incurs extra charges
vocabulary.example: We had to pay for excess baggage at the airport.

vocabulary.meaning: An official message telling passengers to board their plane
vocabulary.example: Listen for the boarding announcement in the lounge.

vocabulary.meaning: A visa allowing short stay while connecting to another flight
vocabulary.example: You may need a transit visa for that stopover.

vocabulary.meaning: The path a plane takes from departure to destination
vocabulary.example: The flight route goes over the Arctic.

vocabulary.meaning: A company that provides air transport services
vocabulary.example: Which airline are you flying with?

vocabulary.meaning: Irregular air movements that cause a bumpy flight
vocabulary.translation: nhieu loan khi quyen / may bay bi xoc
vocabulary.example: Please fasten your seatbelt due to turbulence.

vocabulary.meaning: The area at the front of a plane where the pilot sits
vocabulary.example: Passengers are not allowed in the cockpit.

vocabulary.meaning: The main body of an airplane
vocabulary.example: The fuselage was painted in the airline's colors.

vocabulary.meaning: A large building where aircraft are stored and maintained
vocabulary.example: The plane was towed into the hangar for repairs.

vocabulary.meaning: When a plane moves slowly on the ground before takeoff or after landing
vocabulary.example: The aircraft spent ten minutes taxiing to the runway.

vocabulary.meaning: The height of a plane above sea level
vocabulary.example: The plane reached a cruising altitude of 35,000 feet.

vocabulary.meaning: A second flight that continues a journey after a stop
vocabulary.example: I have a connecting flight in Singapore.

vocabulary.meaning: A flight that goes directly to the destination without stopping
vocabulary.example: We booked a nonstop flight to London.

vocabulary.meaning: The specific gate assigned for boarding a particular flight
vocabulary.example: The boarding gate has been changed to C5.

vocabulary.meaning: A screen showing flight departure information
vocabulary.example: Check the departure board for your gate number.

vocabulary.meaning: The most luxurious and expensive seating class on a plane
vocabulary.example: First class passengers enjoy private suites.

vocabulary.meaning: An enclosed walkway from the terminal to the aircraft door
vocabulary.translation: ong long di bo len may bay
vocabulary.example: We walked through the jet bridge to board the plane.

vocabulary.meaning: The passenger area inside an airplane
vocabulary.example: The cabin crew prepared for landing.

vocabulary.meaning: Goods transported by aircraft in the hold
vocabulary.example: The plane carried both passengers and cargo.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of adding fuel to an aircraft
vocabulary.example: The plane stopped for refueling during the long journey.

vocabulary.meaning: The service that manages the movement of aircraft
vocabulary.example: Air traffic control cleared the plane for takeoff.

vocabulary.meaning: An automatic system that controls the aircraft during flight
vocabulary.example: The pilot switched on the autopilot at cruising altitude.

vocabulary.meaning: The maximum weight or number of bags permitted on a flight
vocabulary.translation: hanh ly cho phep mang theo
vocabulary.example: The baggage allowance for economy is 23 kilograms.

vocabulary.meaning: The steps followed when passengers board an aircraft
vocabulary.example: The boarding procedure starts with families and priority passengers.

vocabulary.meaning: A free meal provided on a flight
vocabulary.translation: bua an mien phi tren may bay
vocabulary.example: Long-haul flights usually include a complimentary meal.

vocabulary.meaning: To leave an aircraft after it has landed
vocabulary.example: Passengers began to disembark after the plane stopped.

vocabulary.meaning: A door on a plane used in an emergency for evacuation
vocabulary.example: Please locate the nearest emergency exit.

vocabulary.meaning: Movies, music, and games available on a plane
vocabulary.example: The in-flight entertainment system has over 200 movies.

vocabulary.meaning: A safety jacket that keeps a person afloat in water
vocabulary.example: The life vest is located under your seat.

vocabulary.meaning: A flight that covers a very long distance
vocabulary.example: A long-haul flight from Vietnam to the US takes about 20 hours.

vocabulary.meaning: A flight that covers a short distance
vocabulary.example: The short-haul flight to Bangkok takes two hours.

vocabulary.meaning: The area in an airport where passengers wait before boarding
vocabulary.example: We relaxed in the departure lounge before our flight.

vocabulary.meaning: An electronic version of a plane ticket stored digitally
vocabulary.example: Show your e-ticket on your phone at check-in.

vocabulary.meaning: Small bags that passengers take into the cabin
vocabulary.example: Hand luggage must fit under the seat or in the overhead bin.

vocabulary.meaning: An official who checks travelers' passports and visas
vocabulary.example: The immigration officer asked about the purpose of my visit.

vocabulary.meaning: A device used at security to detect metal objects
vocabulary.example: Walk through the metal detector slowly.

vocabulary.meaning: An airspace where aircraft are not permitted to fly
vocabulary.example: The military declared a no-fly zone over the area.

vocabulary.meaning: The enclosed storage space above the seats in a plane
vocabulary.example: Place your bag in the overhead compartment.

vocabulary.meaning: A mask that provides oxygen during an emergency on a plane
vocabulary.example: In case of emergency, oxygen masks will drop down automatically.

vocabulary.meaning: A person traveling on an airplane
vocabulary.example: All passengers must board before the doors close.

vocabulary.meaning: To push a seat backward into a more comfortable position
vocabulary.example: Please do not recline your seat during meals.

vocabulary.meaning: A flight that departs late at night and arrives early in the morning
vocabulary.example: I took a red-eye flight to save a night at the hotel.

vocabulary.meaning: A journey to a destination and back to the starting point
vocabulary.example: A round trip ticket costs less than two one-way tickets.

vocabulary.meaning: A ticket for travel in one direction only
vocabulary.example: I bought a one-way ticket because I have no return date.

vocabulary.meaning: Waiting for an available seat on a flight without a confirmed booking
vocabulary.example: We were on standby and luckily got seats on the next flight.

vocabulary.meaning: A planned break in a journey, usually lasting a day or more
vocabulary.example: We had a two-day stopover in Dubai on our way to London.

vocabulary.meaning: A numbered group that determines your boarding order
vocabulary.example: Boarding zone 1 passengers may now proceed to the gate.

vocabulary.meaning: The registration number displayed on the tail of an aircraft
vocabulary.example: The tail number identifies each individual aircraft.

vocabulary.meaning: A toilet on an airplane
vocabulary.translation: nha ve sinh tren may bay
vocabulary.example: The lavatory is located at the rear of the aircraft.

vocabulary.meaning: The second pilot who assists the captain in flying the plane
vocabulary.example: The co-pilot took over the controls during the break.

vocabulary.meaning: A flight arranged for a specific group rather than scheduled publicly
vocabulary.example: The company arranged a charter flight for the employees.

vocabulary.meaning: A flight within the same country
vocabulary.example: Domestic flights do not require a passport.

vocabulary.meaning: A flight that travels between two different countries
vocabulary.example: International flights depart from Terminal 2.

vocabulary.meaning: The science or practice of travel through the air
vocabulary.example: She studied aeronautics at university before becoming a pilot.

vocabulary.meaning: The area of an airport where aircraft are parked or loaded
vocabulary.example: Aircraft are serviced on the apron between flights.

vocabulary.meaning: A wind blowing in the same direction as the aircraft, helping it fly faster
vocabulary.example: A strong tail wind cut 30 minutes off the flight time.

quiz.question: What document do you need to board an airplane?
Where do you collect your bags after landing?
What is the person who serves you on the plane called?
Where do officials check goods entering a country?
What is another word for bags and suitcases?
What do you call a stop between connecting flights?
What is the storage above seats on a plane?
What is the area at the front of the plane where the pilot sits?
What type of flight goes directly to the destination without stopping?
What is the screen showing flight departure information called?
What safety equipment drops from above your seat in an emergency?
What is a flight that departs late at night called?
What is the maximum luggage weight you can bring on a flight?
What is the strip of ground where planes take off and land?
What do you call a flight within the same country?
